results.title: Former British Steel owner demands compensation
results.description: British Steel’s former Chinese owner is demanding compensation after the Government shifted the operator into public ownership. Jingye Group is threatening legal action citing Westminster’s “trampling on international investment rules”. The Government last week nationalised British Steel in a move it says will protect “the future of steel production”. It came after ministers... Read more

results.title: What Language Is Spoken in Paraguay? Guaraní, Spanish and Jopara
results.description: Paraguay has two official languages, Spanish and Guaraní, and about 90% of people speak Guaraní. Here is who speaks what, where, and why it matters. The post What Language Is Spoken in Paraguay? Guaraní, Spanish and Jopara appeared first on The Rio Times .

results.title: In the Israeli-Occupied West Bank, Too Much Cash Is Breaking the Economy
results.description: Picture this: A gas station customer pulls out a wad of bills to pay for a fill-up. The cashier refuses the money, explaining that the station's bank has stopped accepting notes and coins as deposits because it already is drowning in cash. In most places, cash is king. But in the Israeli-occupied West Bank, having too much is gumming up the machinery of daily life. The Palestinian banking system has more Israeli shekels than it can manage. As a result, paper money has gotten harder for residents to spend and for businesses to put into their accounts.

results.title: HMRC's biggest Self Assessment shake-up in decades could change how millions pay tax
results.description: HMRC is consulting on plans to make millions of self-employed workers, landlords and side hustlers pay tax earlier through major Self Assessment reforms.
